taxonomy: Morongia is classified as a genus within the family Campanulaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as bellflowers. This family is part of the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that also includes sunflowers and daisies. The Campanulaceae family is native to the Northern Hemisphere and is characterized by its bell-shaped flowers. Morongia is a relatively small genus, containing a limited number of species found primarily in the tropical regions of the Americas. The classification of Morongia reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the broader Campanulaceae family.
